title: Creating a Bar Graph from Data

steps.instruction: The data: Cats=6, Dogs=10, Fish=4. Choose a scale.
steps.explanation: The scale must reach the highest value.

steps.work: X-axis: 'Pet', Y-axis: 'Number of students'. Title: 'Favorite Pets'
steps.explanation: Always label both axes and add a title.

steps.instruction: Draw each bar to the correct height.
steps.work: Cats bar → 6 (3rd line), Dogs bar → 10 (5th line), Fish bar → 4 (2nd line)
steps.explanation: Each bar should reach exactly the right value on the scale.

steps.instruction: Verify each bar matches the data.
steps.explanation: Always double-check bar heights against your data.

steps.instruction: Data: Mon=15, Tue=25, Wed=35, Thu=20, Fri=30.
steps.explanation: The scale must cover 0 to at least 35.

steps.work: By 1s: too many lines (35). By 5s: 0,5,10,...,35 = 7 lines (good). By 10s: 0,10,20,30,40 = 4 lines (OK but hard to read 15 and 25).
steps.explanation: By 5s gives enough detail to read values accurately.
This scale is detailed enough to show all values clearly.
